data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T154927-930e9b04/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T154927-930e9b04/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T154927-930e9b04/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T154927-930e9b04/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T154927-930e9b04/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-6363480f">
    <title>skills: upgrade human-writing-support workflow examples</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Agents should use the prose workflow without being reminded. The human-writing-support skill needs examples and workflow steps that match the Vale-backed `ddx doc prose` command.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:193-262 defines the skill trigger, workflow, preservation rules, examples, and evals, but the active and shipped skill copies need to be upgraded after the Vale-backed command exists.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Skill behavior is high-judgment because it controls how agents apply prose findings. A weak skill will either ignore findings or over-edit technical docs, commands, tables, IDs, and acceptance criteria.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update active and shipped `human-writing-support` skill copies.
- Add examples for unsupported claims, AI-slop paragraphs, token-cost edits, false positives, and table/path/API preservation.
- Instruct agents to run and rerun `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Add or update skill eval prompts if the repo supports them.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Vale rule semantics.
- Do not make prose findings blocking.
- Do not edit unrelated skills.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed public command.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `ddx skills check .agents/skills/human-writing-support .claude/skills/human-writing-support` passes.
2. `make skill-schema` passes if any `SKILL.md` file is edited.
3. `docs-edit-runs-check` eval or equivalent verifies the skill runs `ddx doc prose --changed` after docs edits.
4. `preserves-technical-structure` eval or equivalent verifies paths, commands, tables, IDs, and AC remain intact.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/skills/... green if skill validation code changes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>adr:025, area:docs, area:skills, kind:implementation, phase:2, prose-quality, spec:PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration, tier:smart</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" base-rev="3b94e7635c8de7edff366f25bd4565b01ecffef7" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T154927-930e9b04"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" path="docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md">Plan: Prose Quality Integration with Vale</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

provider bragi known unreachable (last dial failure 15s ago)
model "anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5" has no catalog power metadata

data.output:
Commit landed; all six ACs verified:

- AC1 `ddx skills check` on both active paths → `validated 2 skill files`
- AC2 `make skill-schema` → `validated 16 skill files`
- AC3/AC4 `TestHumanWritingSupportSkillContent` (docs-edit-runs-check + preserves-technical-structure equivalent) asserts every copy contains `ddx doc prose --changed` and the preservation surface (Paths, commands, table, IDs, acceptance criteria)
- AC5 `go test ./internal/skills/...` green
- AC6 lefthook pre-commit green
